title: Token Forge
subtitle: Smart Contract Generator & ERC-20 Dashboard
overview: Token Forge is a no-code smart contract generator that enables users to deploy ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 tokens across multiple EVM-compatible chains without writing a single line of Solidity.
problem: Launching a token required deep Solidity expertise, auditing knowledge, and familiarity with multiple blockchain explorers. Non-technical founders and communities had no accessible way to tokenize assets or create community tokens.
solution: A visual drag-and-drop interface that generates production-ready, OpenZeppelin-audited smart contracts. Users configure token parameters through a wizard and deploy with one click to any supported EVM chain.

features.title: Visual Contract Builder
features.description: Step-by-step wizard for configuring token name, symbol, supply, decimals, mintability, burnability, and pausability through an intuitive UI.

features.title: Multi-Chain Deployment
features.description: Deploy to Ethereum, Polygon, BNB Chain, Avalanche, and Base with automatic RPC switching and gas estimation before deployment.

features.title: Built-in Audit Checklist
features.description: Pre-deployment security scan that checks for common vulnerabilities, checks-effects-interactions patterns, and reentrancy guards.

features.title: Dashboard & Analytics
features.description: Post-deployment dashboard showing holder distribution, transfer volume, top holders, and contract interactions in real-time.

architecture.title: Monorepo with Off-Chain/On-Chain Separation
architecture.description: Token Forge uses a monorepo structure separating the Next.js frontend, Hardhat contract compilation pipeline, and Graph indexing layer. Smart contracts follow the factory pattern, deploying user tokens through a proxy registry.
highlights: Factory contract pattern for gas-efficient deployment
OpenZeppelin Defender for admin key management
The Graph hosted service for on-chain data indexing
CID-based versioning for contract metadata on IPFS

results.description: Unique wallets that deployed at least one contract
Average gas optimization vs. naive OpenZeppelin implementations
